perturbation.original_text: VIII. Indemnity Each party agrees to indemnify and save harmless the other party against any and all losses, damages, claims, or expenses incurred or suffered as a result of the other party's breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: VIII. Indemnity Each party agrees to indemnify and save harmless the other party against any and all losses, damages, claims, or expenses incurred or suffered as a result of the other party's breach of this Agreement, unless the breach was unintentional.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'unless the breach was unintentional,' a contradiction arises with Section VII (Enforcement). Section VII states damages may not be an adequate remedy. This means that for any breach, intentional or not, enforcement through injunction is possible. But Section VIII now limits indemnity to intentional breaches, in contradiction with the possibility of enforcement in Section VII.
perturbation.contradicted_text: VII. Enforcement Each party acknowledges and agrees that damages may not be an adequate remedy to compensate the other party for any breach of the obligations contained in this Agreement and, accordingly, agrees, that in addition to any other remedies available, the disclosing party shall be entitled to obtain relief by way of temporary or permanent injunction to enforce the obligations contained in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section VIII provides indemnity for any breach of the Agreement. The changed text adds a condition: indemnity is not provided for unintentional breaches. Section VII allows for injunctive relief for *any* breach. If an unintentional breach occurs, Section VII allows the party to seek an injunction, but Section VIII prevents them from seeking indemnity. This creates a direct conflict: Section VII suggests remedies are available regardless of intent, while the modified Section VIII limits indemnity based on intent. Therefore, there is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: I. Confidential Information For purposes of this Agreement, "Confidential Information" shall mean the information, data, know-how, trade secrets, strategic and development plans, business plans, co-developer identities, data, business records, 31 <PAGE> customer lists, any and all versions of software and related documentation, and all other information and documentation which is related to ImagicTV's DTV Manager System and NBTel's systems and operations which may be disclosed between each party or to which either party may be provided access by the other party or others in accordance with this Agreement, or which is generated as a result of or in connection with the Program, which is not generally available to the public.
perturbation.changed_text: I. Confidential Information For purposes of this Agreement, "Confidential Information" shall mean only the explicitly marked information as confidential, data, know-how, trade secrets, strategic and development plans, business plans, co-developer identities, data, business records, 31 <PAGE> customer lists, any and all versions of software and related documentation, and all other information and documentation which is related to ImagicTV's DTV Manager System and NBTel's systems and operations which may be disclosed between each party or to which either party may be provided access by the other party or others in accordance with this Agreement, or which is not generally available to the public.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'only the explicitly marked information as confidential' to the definition of 'Confidential Information' in Section I introduces a contradiction. Section IV (Nondisclosure Obligations) refers to 'Confidential Information' without specifying any marking requirement. The contradiction created is whether all the information is confidential as mentioned in Section IV or only what is explicitly marked in section I. Therefore, there is an uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: IV. Nondisclosure Obligations Each party considers all of its Confidential Information and Intellectual Property to be propriety. All of the disclosing party's Confidential Information and Intellectual Property shall at all times, and throughout the world, remain the property of the disclosing party exclusively, and all applicable rights in patents, copyrights and trade secrets shall remain in the disclosing party exclusively. The receiving party promises and agrees to 32 <PAGE> receive and hold the Confidential Information and Intellectual Property in confidence. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the receiving party further promises and agrees: (a) to protect and safeguard the Confidential Information and Intellectual Property against unauthorized use, publication or disclosure; (b) not to use any of the Confidential Information and Intellectual Property except for the Program; (c) not to, directly or indirectly, in any way, reveal, report, publish, disclose, transfer or otherwise use any of the Confidential Information and Intellectual Property except as specifically authorized by disclosing party in accordance with this Agreement. (d) not to use any Confidential Information and Intellectual Property to compete or obtain advantage vis a vis disclosing party in any commercial activity contemplated by the parties in connection with the Program; (e) to restrict access to the Confidential Information and Intellectual Property to those of its officers, directors, and employees who clearly need such access to carry out the Program; (f) to advise each of the persons to whom it provides access to any of the Confidential Information and Intellectual Property, that such persons are strictly prohibited from making any use, publishing or otherwise disclosing to others, or permitting others to use for their benefit or to the detriment of disclosing party, any of the Confidential Information and Intellectual Property, and, upon request of disclosing party, to provide disclosing party with a copy of a written agreement to that effect signed by such persons; and (g) to comply with any reasonable security measures requested in writing by disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information' broadly. The changed text narrows this definition to 'only the explicitly marked information as confidential'. Section IV, the contradicted text, refers to 'Confidential Information' without any marking requirement. This creates a direct contradiction: Section IV assumes all information is confidential, while the changed Section I limits confidentiality to only explicitly marked information. This inconsistency creates uncertainty and potential unenforceability, fulfilling the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: XI. Successors and Assigns. Neither party shall have the right to assign its rights under this Agreement, whether expressly or by operation of law, without the written consent of the other party. This Agreement and the obligations hereunder shall be binding on any successors and permitted assigns.
perturbation.changed_text: XI. Successors and Assigns. Either party shall have the right to assign its rights under this Agreement, whether expressly or by operation of law, with the verbal consent of the other party. This Agreement and the obligations hereunder shall not be binding on any successors and permitted assigns.
perturbation.explanation: The change in section XI introduces two contradictions: It allows assignment with verbal consent, contradicting the need for written consent mentioned earlier, and it states the agreement shall not be binding on successors and assigns. This contradicts the typical legal principle of agreements being binding on successors and assigns, and creates an ambiguity about the extent to which any obligations can be transferred or delegated, including the obligation to protect confidential information mentioned in section IV.
perturbation.contradicted_text: IV. Nondisclosure Obligations Each party considers all of its Confidential Information and Intellectual Property to be propriety. All of the disclosing party's Confidential Information and Intellectual Property shall at all times, and throughout the world, remain the property of the disclosing party exclusively, and all applicable rights in patents, copyrights and trade secrets shall remain in the disclosing party exclusively. The receiving party promises and agrees to 32 <PAGE> receive and hold the Confidential Information and Intellectual Property in confidence. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the receiving party further promises and agrees: (a) to protect and safeguard the Confidential Information and Intellectual Property against unauthorized use, publication or disclosure; (b) not to use any of the Confidential Information and Intellectual Property except for the Program; (c) not to, directly or indirectly, in any way, reveal, report, publish, disclose, transfer or otherwise use any of the Confidential Information and Intellectual Property except as specifically authorized by disclosing party in accordance with this Agreement. (d) not to use any Confidential Information and Intellectual Property to compete or obtain advantage vis a vis disclosing party in any commercial activity contemplated by the parties in connection with the Program; (e) to restrict access to the Confidential Information and Intellectual Property to those of its officers, directors, and employees who clearly need such access to carry out the Program; (f) to advise each of the persons to whom it provides access to any of the Confidential Information and Intellectual Property, that such persons are strictly prohibited from making any use, publishing or otherwise disclosing to others, or permitting others to use for their benefit or to the detriment of disclosing party, any of the Confidential Information and Intellectual Property, and, upon request of disclosing party, to provide disclosing party with a copy of a written agreement to that effect signed by such persons; and (g) to comply with any reasonable security measures requested in writing by disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section XI introduces a contradiction with section IV. Section XI now states that the agreement shall not be binding on any successors and permitted assigns. Section IV outlines nondisclosure obligations and states that the receiving party promises to hold confidential information in confidence. If the agreement is not binding on successors and assigns as per the changed section XI, it creates uncertainty as to whether successors and assigns would be bound by the nondisclosure obligations in Section IV, creating a contradiction and potential unenforceability of the confidential information obligations. Additionally, the change from 'written consent' to 'verbal consent' in Section XI introduces ambiguity and potential enforceability issues, indirectly contradicting the need for clear and documented agreements implicit in Section IV regarding the protection of confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: XIII. Entire Agreement The terms and conditions herein constitute the entire agreement and understanding of the parties and shall supersede all communications, negotiations, arrangements and agreements, either oral or written, with respect to the subject matter hereof. No amendments to or modifications of this Agreement shall be effective unless reduced to writing and executed by the Parties hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: XIII. Entire Agreement The terms and conditions herein constitute the entire agreement and understanding of the parties and shall supersede all communications, negotiations, arrangements and agreements, either oral or written, with respect to the subject matter hereof. Any amendments to or modifications of this Agreement shall be effective either in writing and executed by the Parties hereto or made verbally between the parties involved.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'or made verbally between the parties involved' in Section XIII (Entire Agreement) creates a contradiction. It now states that amendments can be made verbally, directly contradicting the previous statement in the same section that 'No amendments to or modifications of this Agreement shall be effective unless reduced to writing and executed by the Parties hereto.' This also can affect the effectiveness of Section IV (Nondisclosure Obligations) if a verbal modification altered the scope of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: XIII. Entire Agreement The terms and conditions herein constitute the entire agreement and understanding of the parties and shall supersede all communications, negotiations, arrangements and agreements, either oral or written, with respect to the subject matter hereof. Any amendments to or modifications of this Agreement shall be effective either in writing and executed by the Parties hereto or made verbally between the parties involved. The failure of either party to enforce any term hereof shall not be deemed a waiver of any rights contained herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section XIII introduces a direct in-text contradiction. The original text stated that 'No amendments to or modifications of this Agreement shall be effective unless reduced to writing and executed by the Parties hereto.' The changed text adds 'or made verbally between the parties involved.' This directly contradicts the previous statement within the same section, creating a scenario where verbal modifications are deemed effective, conflicting with the requirement for written and executed amendments. This is a clear inconsistency as defined, making it a strong in-text contradiction.
